summary: Tested all 10 features for Forgot Password functionality. All features working correctly: (1) 'Esqueci minha senha' link visible on login screen - PASS, (2) Forgot password form with amber/yellow accent - PASS, (3) Success page with 'E-mail enviado!' - PASS, (4) 'Voltar ao login' button returns to login - PASS, (5) Backend forgot-password returns success for any email - PASS, (6) Backend reset-password with valid token works - PASS, (7) Backend reset-password with invalid token returns error - PASS, (8) Reset password page shows form with ?reset_password=TOKEN - PASS, (9) Password validation (min 6 chars, confirm match) - PASS, (10) Login with new password after reset - PASS
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: ForgotPasswordReq and ResetPasswordReq models properly defined with email/token/new_password fields
server.py: forgot_password endpoint correctly returns success for any email (security best practice - doesn't reveal if email exists)
server.py: reset_password endpoint validates token, checks expiry, validates password length (min 6 chars), and clears token after use
server.py: get_reset_password_email_html generates beautiful HTML email matching dark theme with amber/yellow accent
LoginScreen.jsx: mode='forgot' state properly handles forgot password flow with forgotEmail, forgotSent, forgotLoading states
LoginScreen.jsx: 'Esqueci minha senha' link visible in login mode below Remember Me checkbox
main.jsx: ResetPasswordPage component handles ?reset_password=TOKEN URL param with form validation and error states
context_for_next_testing_agent: Forgot Password functionality is complete and working. The flow is: (1) Click 'Esqueci minha senha' on login screen, (2) Enter email and submit, (3) Success page shows 'E-mail enviado!', (4) User receives email with reset link (note: Resend is in sandbox mode so emails only go to registered Resend account), (5) Click link opens /?reset_password=TOKEN, (6) Enter new password (min 6 chars) and confirm, (7) Password reset success, (8) Login with new password. Backend tests verify token creation, expiry, validation, and cleanup. The email HTML uses amber/yellow accent color matching the app's dark theme.

features_tested.login_screen_forgot_link: PASS - 'Esqueci minha senha' link visible below Remember Me checkbox
features_tested.forgot_password_form: PASS - Form shows with amber/yellow accent button, email input
features_tested.forgot_password_success: PASS - Shows 'E-mail enviado!' with next steps and 'Voltar ao login' button
features_tested.forgot_password_back_button: PASS - Returns to login screen correctly
features_tested.backend_forgot_password_any_email: PASS - Returns success for both existing and non-existing emails (security)
features_tested.backend_reset_password_valid_token: PASS - Resets password and clears token from DB
features_tested.backend_reset_password_invalid_token: PASS - Returns 400 error 'Link inválido ou já utilizado'
features_tested.reset_password_page_ui: PASS - Shows form with 'Nova senha' title, password fields, amber button
features_tested.reset_password_validation: PASS - Min 6 chars validation, password mismatch error shown
features_tested.login_after_reset: PASS - User can login with new password after reset

email_integration_note: Resend email service is in sandbox mode - emails can only be sent to the registered Resend account email (rafah.26ferreira@hotmail.com). The forgot-password endpoint attempts to send email but may fail in sandbox. The API returns success regardless to not reveal if email exists.
